Article Title: SC issues notice to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla against approval of Sena (UBT) MPs' merger with Shinde faction
Article Text:
SC issues notice to Om Birla (ANI images)

Moved to SC earlier

Merger alters Lok Sabha numbers

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Wednesday issued notices to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, the Lok Sabha Secretary and six rebel Shiv Sena MPs on a plea filed by the Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray (UBT) faction challenging the Speaker's decision to recognise the merger of the six MPs with the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena.The top court has further posted the matter for hearing after two weeks.The plea was filed by UBT MP Arvind Ganpat Sawant against the Speaker's decision, which was approved ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament. A bench comprising Justices PS Narasimha and Alok Aradhe issued notice to the respondents while refusing to stay the Speaker's order at this stage.The Shiv Sena (UBT) on Tuesday approached the Supreme Court challenging Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla's decision to approve the merger of its six MPs with the Shiv Sena led by Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde.Senior advocate Devadatt Kamat, appearing for the Uddhav Thackeray-led party, mentioned the matter before a bench comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M. Pancholi, seeking an urgent hearing on Wednesday.Ahead of the Monsoon Session, Speaker Om Birla approved the merger of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs with the Shinde-led Shiv Sena.The MPs are Sanjay Deshmukh (Yavatmal), Sanjay Jadhav (Parbhani), Sanjay Dina Patil (Mumbai North East), Nagesh Patil-Ashtikar (Hingoli), Omprakash Rajenimbalkar (Dharashiv) and Bhausaheb Wakchaure (Shirdi).With the merger, the Uddhav Thackeray-led Shiv Sena's strength in the Lok Sabha has come down to three MPs, while the Shinde-led Shiv Sena's tally has increased to 13 MPs, making it the third-largest constituent of the NDA after the BJP and the Telugu Desam Party (TDP).Reacting to the development, Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde said the six MPs joined his party to secure development for their constituencies and not for "personal gains".
Article Summary: Supreme Court issues notices to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, the Lok Sabha Secretary and six rebel Shiv Sena MPs. Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray (UBT) faction challenging Speaker's decision to recognise the merger of the six MPs with the Eknath Shinde-led ShivSena.

Article Title: Parliament washout again; govt reaches out to opposition to end impasse
Article Text:
Opposition MPs stage protest against govt at Parliament in black clothes

NEW DELHI: Govt and opposition were locked in intense discussions Wednesday to break the deadlock in Parliament, as the aftermath of the police crackdown on student protests led to a hardening of stance by the anti-BJP bloc - with both Houses witnessing a washout of proceedings for the third consecutive day.Opposition is sticking to its demand for education minister Pradhan's resignation - termed "non-negotiable" by Rahul Gandhi and SP chief Akhilesh Yadav. Govt is not ready to accede even as it offers a discussion on paper leaks.Sources said LS Speaker Om Birla reached out to Congress' K C Venugopal while parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju called on Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge. Opposition said a final view will be taken at a meeting of parties in Kharge's chamber Thursday morning.Sources privy to the discussion feel that among the options a compromise is possible on an adjournment motion — discussion under Rule 56 in Lok Sabha and Rule 267 in Rajya Sabha.Amid the deadlock, LS and RS failed to transact any business for the third day. In the lower House, Congress’s Venugopal demanded Pradhan’s resignation, even as he sought a discussion under the adjournment motion. But Union minister Rijiju merely reiterated govt’s willingness to hold a discussion, with the rule to be decided by the speaker and the parties.Amid the back and forth, Akhilesh expressed anguish that while BJP and Congress members are allowed to speak, he does not get a chance to air his views. He asked if a deal has been reached, asserting that the issue is not about political parties but about students. The feeling in opposition camp is that BJP is on the defensive, and they should leverage the situation to maximum.At the same time, the opposition is wary of coming across as letting down the students. There are multiple players ranging from the political class to civil society to Sonam Wangchuk in the mix. As a result, a joint stand will be taken by opposition parties only after considering all the possible options.The difficulty in reaching a middle ground was visible through the day as the leader of the opposition in Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi, attacked the Modi govt, drawing a furious response from Union health minister and Leader of Rajya Sabha J P Nadda. Rahul said while police were dragging him away outside the PM’s residence on Tuesday, they were whispering in his ears “remove this govt”.
Article Summary: LS Speaker Om Birla reached out to Congress' K C Venugopal while parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju called on Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge. Opposition sticking to its demand for education minister Pradhan's resignation - termed "non-negotiable" by Rahul Gandhi and Akhilesh Yadav.

Article Title: Congress protests: Rahul digs in, Nadda says selective outrage
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi calls education system 'rigged', Nadda accuses Oppn of seeking 'political mileage'

NEW DELHI: Congress MP Rahul Gandhi Wednesday said the sacking of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan is a must to end the confrontation triggered by the Monday crackdown on protesting students, lobbing a challenge to the Modi govt. He further demanded that PM Modi apologise to the country's youth and action be taken against the persons responsible for violence against the protestors.Rahul listed the three demands as "non-negotiable" to defuse the escalating tensions that have spilled over from restive youngsters to the political domain, turning national focus to the single issue spotlighted by the Jantar Mantar protest of Cockroach Janata Party (CJP).Addressing a news conference, Rahul said govt is in panic and its brass has collapsed psychologically. "We fully support every single student who is on the streets." He accused govt of "rigging" and destroying the education system which was considered among the best in the world.He said students are being put through unlimited stress because of "paper leaks", claiming the number of known cases of leaks is "152 in the last decade" - which means one leak a month.In contrast, Rahul said there has not been a single conviction and that 7.5 crore families have suffered because of these leaks.Rahul said the crisis of the system is not just paper leaks but also that the system has become unaffordable for the poor and the middle class. "One exam (NEET) costs the families the same money as our entire education budget. The govt spends Rs 1.4 lakh crore on education, and Rs 1.32 lakh crore is spent by families who take the NEET exam. This is pure theft from these families." Paper leaks have hurt the youngsters badly because govt jobs are the only option left to them since the manufacturing sector and private sector have nosedived and entrepreneurship, which requires loans, is limited to big businesses, Rahul said. He dismissed questions about his treatment by police during the dharna outside the PM residence as "irrelevant" to the central issue.
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi calls education system 'rigged', Nadda accuses Oppn of seeking 'political mileage' Gandhi demands sacking of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan. He also demands that PM Modi apologise to the country's youth.

Article Title: Amid mergers, Sibal urges SC to interpret law on defection curbs
Article Text:
MP Kapil Sibal personally entered the legal battlefield

NEW DELHI: After being the counsel for Sena UBT contesting the defection of Shiv Sena MLAs led by Eknath Shinde which resulted in the collapse of MVA govt in Maharashtra, senior advocate and MP Kapil Sibal Wednesday personally entered the legal battlefield by petitioning SC seeking interpretation of anti-defection law in order to curb illegal defections and mergers."I have filed a petition in person. The central issue in the petition is whether the composition of Parliament can change in the fashion in which it is happening in this country. The interpretation of the 10th Schedule's fourth paragraph (which permits mergers) is the need of the hour. A similar petition is pending," Sibal told a bench led by CJI Surya Kant and sought urgent listing. SC agreed to list the matter on Monday.Sena UBT has also moved a petition questioning the Lok Sabha Speaker's decision to allow merger of six of its nine MPs with the rival Sena party led by Shinde. SC Wednesday entertained the petition and sought responses from the Centre and LS secretariat.In his petition, Sibal cited several instances of defection and subsequent merger of rebel MLAs and MPs, including those affecting Sena UBT and Trinamool Congress.The fourth paragraph of the Tenth Schedule carves out an exception to disqualification for defections.
Article Summary: MP Kapil Sibal files petition seeking interpretation of anti-defection law to curb illegal defections and mergers. Sibal was counsel for Sena UBT contesting the defection of Shiv Sena MLAs which resulted in the collapse of MVA govt in Maharashtra.

Article Title: SC seeks Speaker response to UBT plea against MPs' merger with Sena
Article Text:
SC agreed to test the validity of Lok Sabha Speaker’s decision to recognise the merger of six Sena UBT MPs with the rival EknathShinde-led faction

NEW DELHI: Supreme Court on Wednesday agreed to test the validity of Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla's decision to recognise the merger of six Sena UBT MPs with the rival Eknath Shinde-led faction on a plea filed by Sena UBT, alleging that it amounts to creating a constitutional hara-kiri, and weakens the very foundation of parliamentary democracy.A bench of Justices P S Narasimha and Alok Aradhe issued notice to the office of LS Speaker and six rebel MPs seeking their response on a petition filed by Sena UBT MP, Arvind Sawant. The top court sought their response and posted the case after two weeks, when it would consider interim prayer for staying the order.Senior advocate Devadatta Kamat, appearing for the petitioner, submitted that the case involves important questions of constitutional morality and propriety and the whole thing was "orchestrated" only for the purposes of Parliament session.The petition said the legislators cannot decide on a merger with another political party. "In the present case, the original political party has categorically asserted that there is no merger; therefore, the Speaker could not have permitted a group of legislators, irrespective of their numerical strength, to merge into another political party," it added.
Article Summary: Supreme Court agreed to test the validity of Lok Sabha Speaker’s decision to recognise the merger of six Sena UBT MPs with the rival Eknath Shinde-led faction. A bench of Justices P S Narasimha and Alok Aradhe issued notice to the office of LS Speaker and six rebel MPs seeking their response.

Article Title: Watch: TMC MLA Kunal Ghosh marshalled out, suspended after assembly ruckus
Article Text:
The Speaker ordered Assembly marshals to remove Kunal Ghosh from the House

Marshals remove Ghosh after disruption in House

Internal TMC rift spills onto Assembly floor

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress (TMC) MLA Kunal Ghosh was suspended from the West Bengal Assembly for the remainder of Wednesday after he disrupted proceedings by interrupting rebel TMC Chief Whip Akhruzzaman during a debate on the Home and Hill Affairs budget.The suspension came after marshals forcibly escorted Ghosh out of the House when he refused to return to his seat despite repeated directions from the Speaker.The dramatic scenes unfolded amid an escalating power struggle between rival factions within the TMC legislature party.The disruption began when Akhruzzaman, who belongs to the Ritabrata Banerjee-led rebel faction of the TMC, rose to speak during the debate.Ghosh walked up to the microphone and interrupted him, questioning why his own name had allegedly been removed from the list of speakers.Speaker Rathindra Bose repeatedly appealed to Ghosh to return to his seat, while senior leaders also attempted to defuse the situation.However, after Ghosh refused to comply, the Speaker ordered Assembly marshals to remove him from the House. Ghosh resisted, leading to a brief scuffle before marshals carried him out, triggering protests inside the Assembly.Industries minister Tapas Roy initially proposed that Ghosh be suspended for the remainder of the ongoing Assembly session. However, chief minister Suvendu Adhikari suggested that suspending him for the day would be sufficient."This type of behaviour is not acceptable. We are also in an opposition post, but there is some responsibility. Akhruzzaman is a senior MLA and his father was also a long-time MLA," Adhikari said.The Speaker subsequently placed the proposal before the House, and Ghosh was suspended for the rest of the day through a voice vote, with support from BJP legislators as well as members of the rebel TMC faction.The incident highlighted the deepening divide between the Mamata Banerjee-led 'Kalighat' faction, to which Ghosh belongs, and the Ritabrata Banerjee-led rebel camp within the TMC legislature party.Ghosh alleged that his name had been removed from the list of speakers despite being nominated to participate in the debate on the Home and Hill Affairs budget.He claimed the move prompted his protest inside the House.The confrontation comes a day after disagreements over the Opposition's list of speakers exposed tensions within the TMC legislature party.Legislators aligned with the Kalighat faction had alleged that names recommended by them were being dropped from the speaking list, reflecting an ongoing battle over floor coordination and control within the party.Assembly proceedings resumed only after Ghosh was removed from the House.
Article Summary: Kunal Ghosh was suspended from the West Bengal Assembly for the remainder of Wednesday. He disrupted proceedings by interrupting rebel TMC Chief Whip Akhruzzaman during a debate on the Home and Hill Affairs budget. The suspension came after marshals forcibly escorted Ghosh out of the House.

Article Title: 'Used to getting punched': Rahul Gandhi says he is 'ready to take many more' after detention during NEET protest
Article Text:
'Used to getting punched': Rahul Gandhi says he is 'ready to take many more' after detention during NEET protest

NEW DELHI: Congress MP and Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi on Wednesday said he was ‘used to getting punched’ and was ready to ‘take many more’ while standing with students protesting over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak and demanding reforms in the education system.His remarks came a day after he, along with Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav and other opposition leaders was detained by Delhi Police during a sit-in outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence over the issue.Addressing a press conference at the AICC headquarters, Gandhi said standing up for students, farmers and young people was part of his responsibility as the Leader of the Opposition.Responding to a question about his detention and the police action during Tuesday's protest, Gandhi said, "It's fine. I'm used to getting punched and all. It's no problem. My job is to express the will of India's people, whether it is the will of the students, whether it is the will of young people, whether it is the will of the farmers. This is the job that I am supposed to do as leader of opposition."He added, "I am supposed to become an expression of the voice of India's people.I am supposed to bring attention to where pain is being felt. These are very small costs. These are not big costs. It's a huge honour for me to do this, to be able to stand with the wishes of my people. So couple of punches and all. This is not a problem at all. I'm happy to take many more."Replying to another question about what happened outside the Prime Minister's residence, Gandhi said, "My job is to express the will of India's people. Whether it is the will of the students, the young people, or the farmers, this is the job I am supposed to do as the Leader of the Opposition. I am supposed to become an expression of the voice of India's people. I am supposed to bring attention to where pain is being felt. These are very small costs, not big ones. It is a huge honour for me to do this, to be able to stand with the wishes of my people. So a couple of punches and all, that is not a problem at all. I am happy to take many more."During the press conference, Gandhi reiterated his criticism of the Centre over the alleged examination irregularities, accusing the government of destroying India's education system and expressing full support for the protesting students.He said students were demanding a fair education system and backed their demand for the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Gandhi also demanded that those responsible for the alleged police action against students be held accountable and said Prime Minister Narendra Modi should apologise to them.He claimed that 152 paper leaks had taken place over the past decade with 'zero convictions', alleged that 7.5 crore students and their families had been affected, and said the country's education system had become "rigged" and unaffordable.Rahul Gandhi's remarks came a day after he, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, Akhilesh Yadav and several opposition leaders staged a sit-in outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence, demanding a discussion on the alleged NEET paper leak, the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan and action over the police crackdown on students.Delhi Police later removed and detained the leaders after the protest entered the high-security zone. Rahul Gandhi was taken to Chhatrasal Stadium before being released a few hours later, while Priyanka Gandhi and some Congress MPs were taken to Mandir Marg police station and later released.The Congress also released videos and photographs showing Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi being removed by police during the protest.One video showed Rahul Gandhi lying on the road as police personnel attempted to carry him away, while another appeared to show him being dragged during the detention. The party also shared a photograph that appeared to show him with a nosebleed following the police action.The Congress has accused the government of suppressing protests over the alleged examination irregularities, while the Centre has said it was willing to discuss the issue in Parliament and criticised the opposition's protest outside the PM Modi's residence.
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi says he is'ready to take many more' after detention during NEET protest. His remarks came a day after he, along with Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav and other opposition leaders was detained.

Article Title: 'It is a party of barely 20 people': Kalyan Banerjee hits out at TMC rebels
Article Text:
Kalyan Banerjee hits out at TMC rebels after Lok Sabha suspension

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee on Wednesday lashed out at the rebel Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) faction after he was suspended from the Lok Sabha for the remainder of the Monsoon Session, calling its members ‘traitors’ and questioning what his suspension would achieve.His remarks came after the Lok Sabha suspended him for the rest of the session over allegations of indecent behaviour and the use of ‘unsavoury’ and misogynistic language against women MPs following a heated exchange with NCPI members inside the House.Speaking to reporters after his suspension, Banerjee questioned the move and targeted the rebel MPs.“What will they get by suspending me from here?... It is a party of barely twenty people. They (20 TMC rebel MPs) held a meeting in Kolkata after a month and a half. They do not even have twenty voters. They have nothing. Not a single person has voted for them in West Bengal... They are all traitors and dishonest people... The government will fall after a year,” Banerjee said.Union law minister Arjun Ram Meghwal moved a motion in the Lok Sabha seeking Banerjee's suspension after the Speaker received a written complaint alleging that the TMC leader had displayed ‘unprecedented behaviour’ and used objectionable and misogynistic language against women MPs, including NCPI members and Union minister Bhupendra Yadav.The motion was adopted by a voice vote, following which Banerjee was suspended for the remainder of the Monsoon Session, which is scheduled to conclude on August 13.Earlier in the day, Banerjee was involved in a heated exchange with several of his former party colleagues, including NCPI MPs Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, Mitali Bagh and Shatabdi Roy, inside the Lok Sabha chamber after proceedings were adjourned. Other opposition MPs intervened to defuse the situation before the NCPI members later approached Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla over the incident.The suspension follows weeks of controversy surrounding Banerjee's alleged conduct in Parliament. In June, NCPI leader and former TMC MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar had written to Speaker Om Birla seeking his expulsion from the Lok Sabha, accusing him of repeatedly using objectionable, disrespectful and misogynistic language against women MPs.Dastidar had described Banerjee as a ‘habitual offender’ and demanded action, saying no member should be allowed to repeatedly insult women inside Parliament.The latest confrontation also comes against the backdrop of a split within the Trinamool Congress after 20 MPs broke away from the party following its defeat in the West Bengal assembly elections and joined the NCPI.The rebel MPs now sit separately in the Lok Sabha and have declared support for the NDA government and Prime Minister Narendra Modi, while Banerjee has remained with the Mamata Banerjee-led TMC.
Article Summary: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee lashed out at rebel Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) faction. His remarks came after the Lok Sabha suspended him over allegations of indecent behaviour and the use of ‘unsavoury’ and misogynistic language against women MPs.

Article Title: 'Will see where he is in 2031': Suvendu Adhikari dares Abhishek Banerjee to contest from Diamond Harbour
Article Text:
Poll Vote & Share your view How important is the Diamond Harbour constituency in West Bengal politics? Very important Not important 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: West Bengal chief minister Suvendu Adhikari on Wednesday upped the ante against Trinamool Congress MP Abhishek Banerjee and dared him to contest from the Diamond Harbour constituency in the next Lok Sabha polls.Speaking in the West Bengal assembly, Adhikari launched a veiled attack, referring to the TMC leader's remarks at the Martyrs' Day rally held a day earlier, and claimed the BJP would decide "where he will be" by then."Yesterday, there was a lot of loud talk. Someone asked his party workers to note it down, and that accounts would be settled in 2031. We will see where he is in 2031. If he has the courage, let him contest from the Diamond Harbour Lok Sabha seat," Adhikari said."Talking big with a gathering of 2,500 people serves no purpose. I draw four times that crowd even at a meeting in a single gram panchayat," he added, without naming Abhishek.This comes a day after Abhishek accused the BJP of trying to weaken the party through "betrayal" and vowed that the saffron camp would be politically defeated in the state."What will the BJP do? Put us in jail? Frame us in false cases? Let them. We will never bow our heads because our backbone is not for sale," Abhishek had said."This new Trinamool will continue fighting in Bengal politics for the next 40 years, and in 2031 it will settle every account," he added.Diamond Harbour, represented by Abhishek Banerjee in the Lok Sabha since 2014, has long been regarded as his political stronghold.
Article Summary: West Bengal chief minister Suvendu Adhikari dared Abhishek Banerjee to contest from the Diamond Harbour constituency in the next Lok Sabha polls. He said the BJP would decide "where he will be" by then. Diamond Harbour has long been regarded as his political stronghold. This comes a day after he accused the BJP of trying to weaken the party through "betrayal"

Article Title: 7 FIRs filed for theft of 1,750kg copper coils from MSEDCL transformers in Panvel
Article Text:
The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. (AI-generated image)

NAVI MUMBAI: Panvel City Police have registered seven separate FIRs against unidentified persons for allegedly stealing 1,750 kg of copper coils worth Rs 8.75 lakh from MSEDCL transformers kept at different plots in Karanjade node of Panvel taluka. The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. The incident came to light after MSEDCL officials inspected the plots.According to the complainant, Amit Palve (42), assistant engineer with MSEDCL's Pargaon branch in Panvel taluka, the thieves broke open the windows of rooms at seven different plots where the defunct transformers were kept and stole the copper coils from them.When contacted, Palve said, "The seven transformers were defunct and hence kept in the rooms at the respective plots. On July 6, the theft came to light and we informed the Panvel City Police, who carried out a preliminary investigation. As there was no CCTV surveillance in the area, there was no technical evidence. Since it was a major theft, an FIR was registered against unidentified accused under relevant BNS sections for the offences of trespassing and theft. "
Article Summary: Panvel City Police have registered seven separate FIRs against unidentified persons for allegedly stealing 1,750 kg of copper coils worth Rs 8.75 lakh. The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. The incident came to light after MSEDCL officials inspected the plots.

Article Title: BJYM Nagpur chief slams Cong protest
Article Text: Nagpur: Tension prevailed outside the Congress office at Chitnis Park in Mahal on Wednesday after Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha (BJYM), Nagpur, staged a protest against Rahul Gandhi and the Congress over the party's demonstration regarding the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar, New Delhi.The protest, led by BJYM Nagpur city chief Sachin Karare, witnessed sloganeering by workers of both political parties before police intervened and detained the protesters.BJYM leaders alleged that Rahul Gandhi and Congress workers had attempted to force their way into the Prime Minister's residence during the protest in Delhi and described the incident as an act of "Urban Naxalism." Addressing party workers, the leaders criticised the Congress and demanded accountability, following which protesters marched towards the Congress office, causing havoc.As BJYM workers reached the Congress office, Congress members assembled inside the premises and raised slogans against the BJP-led government, leading to a face-off between supporters of the two parties.According to police, BJYM workers attempted to breach the security barricades and enter the Congress office. Officers intervened, restrained the protesters and detained several workers.
Article Summary: BJYM staged a protest against Rahul Gandhi and the Congress over the party's demonstration regarding the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar, New Delhi. The protest witnessed sloganeering by workers of both political parties before police intervened and detained the protesters.

Article Title: Protests in Nashik, Dhule, Jalgaon seeking Pradhan’s resignation
Article Text:
MNS workers protest against the police lathicharge on agitating students in Delhi

Nashik

:

The ongoing protests in Delhi demanding Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan 's resignation over the NEET paper leak issue echoed across north Maharashtra on Wednesday, with Congress and Maharashtra Navnirman Sena (MNS) workers staging demonstrations in several districts, while BJP launched a counter-protest in Nashik.In Nashik, Congress workers and MNS activists protested against the lathicharge on students demonstrating at Jantar Mantar. BJP, however, accused opposition parties of hijacking the agitation and held a protest outside the Congress office.MNS functionaries staged a demonstration at the district collectorate and also attempted a rasta roko to condemn the police action against students in Delhi. State vice-president Salim Shaikh, Ratankumar Icham, Ankush Pawar and Sudam Kombde were among those who participated in the agitation.Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha (BJYM) protested outside the Congress office, alleging that anti-national and anti-Hindu slogans had been raised during the Delhi demonstrations.BJP Nashik president Sunil Kedar launched a sharp attack on the agitation and the opposition parties backing it. “Cockroach Janta Party, born out of social media abroad, has started an agitation over the paper leak. During this protest, anti-RSS and anti-national slogans were raised.Opposition parties, including Congress and the Samajwadi Party, jumped into this agitation, causing it to deviate from its core issue. Congress and other parties provoked youths who had no connection to this movement to escalate the agitation,” Kedar alleged.Congress workers, led by city president Nilesh Khaire, staged protests at the district collectorate and outside the party office in Nashik, raising slogans against the Delhi Police and BJP over the handling of the student agitation. The police deployed heavy bandobast across the city to prevent any untoward incidents.Similar protests were organised by Congress in Malegaon, Jalgaon and Dhule, while MNS activists also staged demonstrations in Dhule in support of the students' movement.
Article Summary: Congress and Maharashtra Navnirman Sena (MNS) workers staged demonstrations in several districts, while BJP launched a counter-protest in Nashik. MNS functionaries staged a demonstration at the district collectorate and also attempted a rasta roko to condemn the police action against students in Delhi.

Article Title: Fire breaks out at plastic goods warehouse in Chennai’s Pudupet
Article Text:
Fire

Chennai: A minor fire broke out at a plastic goods warehouse on the third floor of a residential building in Pudupet on Wednesday. No casualties were reported.According to officials, the fire erupted at the warehouse on Narayana Street. Residents noticed thick smoke billowing from the building and alerted the fire and rescue services around 1.15pm. The flames spread rapidly through the warehouse, gutting the combustible plastic materials stored inside.Four fire tenders from the Egmore, Triplicane and Kilpauk fire stations were rushed to the scene. Police and fire and rescue services personnel launched an investigation to ascertain the cause of the accident.
Article Summary: A minor fire broke out at a plastic goods warehouse on the third floor of a residential building in Pudupet on Wednesday. Residents noticed thick smoke billowing from the building and alerted the fire and rescue services around 1.15pm. The flames spread rapidly through the warehouse, gutting the combustible plastic materials stored inside.

Article Title: Man trampled to death by elephant near Dharmapuri
Article Text:
Representative image

DHARMAPURI: A 36-year-old man, who had been missing for three days, was found trampled to death by a wild elephant near the Kalappampadi forest area in Dharmapuri district on Wednesday.The deceased has been identified as Subramani of Athimarathur village near Pennagaram. Subramani, son of Chinnasamy and his wife Sivabakkiam, was suffering from mental illness.He went missing on Sunday, and family members and relatives searched for him. On Wednesday, they found his dead body near a farm well. Villagers informed forest and police officials, who reached the spot, recovered the body and sent it to Dharmapuri Govt Medical College and Hospital for postmortem. Investigations revealed that he had been trampled to death by an elephant, police said.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as Subramani of Athimarathur village near Pennagaram. He went missing on Sunday, and family members and relatives searched for him. Investigations revealed that he had been trampled to death by an elephant, police said.

Article Title: Man dies after being attacked by wife near Coimbatore
Article Text: COIMBATORE: A 53-year-old vegetable seller died in Coimbatore Medical College Hospital on Monday night after he was assaulted by his wife during a drunken quarrel near Sirumugai in Coimbatore district.The deceased has been identified as K Chenniappan, 53, of Periyur in Palathottam. He was selling vegetables on a pushcart. His wife, Pushpalatha, 47, also sold vegetables using a pushcart. The couple have two sons.According to police sources, on July 17 night, Chenniappan, who was in an inebriated state, went to his wife’s vegetable pushcart, picked up a fight with her and assaulted her. Enraged, Pushpalatha picked up a log from the ground and struck him on the head.Chenniappan, who sustained severe head injuries, was rushed to Mettupalayam govt hospital. Later, he transferred to Coimbatore Medical College Hospital for further treatment. However, he died on Monday night without responding to treatment.The Sirumugai police registered a case and arrested Pushpalatha.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as K Chenniappan, 53, of Periyur in Palathottam. He was selling vegetables on a pushcart. His wife, Pushpalatha, 47, also sold vegetables using a push cart. The couple have two sons.

Article Title: Two men arrested in connection with coworker’s death near Coimbatore
Article Text:
Representative image

COIMBATORE: The Sirumugai police in Coimbatore district have arrested two guest workers in connection with the death of a 27-year-old man who was their coworker.The deceased has been identified as Pisi Yadav, 27, from Bihar, who was employed at a private firm in Jadayampalayam near Sirumugai.Police initially registered a case under Section 194 of the BNSS for suspicious death, based on a complaint lodged by the victim’s brother, Roshan Kumar, 21.According to police, Pisi Yadav was involved in a quarrel with his co-workers on July 16. He was later admitted to govt hospital in Mettupalayam, with claims that he had sustained injuries in a road accident. On July 18, Roshan Kumar was informed that his brother had died without responding to treatment. He lodged a complaint with the Sirumugai police on July 18.During investigations, police questioned two of the deceased’s co-workers, Ujith, 43, and Ajith Jamadar, 38. Both are natives of Bihar. Inquiries revealed that the suspects allegedly assaulted Pisi Yadav in their residential quarters in the early hours of July 17 following a dispute over repayment of money. He was later taken to hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries.Based on the evidence collected, police altered the case on July 19 from suspicious death under Section 194 BNSS to Section 105 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) of the BNS. Both were remanded in judicial custody.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as Pisi Yadav, 27, from Bihar. He was employed at a private firm in Jadayampalayam near Sirumugai. Police questioned two of the deceased’s co-workers, Ujith, 43, and Ajith Jamadar, 38.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: HC dismisses anticipatory bail plea of accused in Palani mutt land scam case
Article Text: Madras high court on Wednesday dismissed the anticipatory bail plea of K Vellathurai of Tirupur
Article Summary: Madras high court dismisses anticipatory bail plea of K Vellathurai of Tirupur. Vellithurai is accused of raping a woman in a hotel room in Chennai. He is also accused of assaulting another woman in the same hotel room.

Article Title: 15 injured in fireworks blast during church festival in Dindigul
Article Text:
As many as 15 people sustained injuries while crackers were burst

Madurai:

As many as 15 people sustained injuries while crackers were burst during the annual festival of St Mary Magdalene Church in Vakkampatti, Dindigul district, on Tuesday midnight.The festival commenced with the flag-hoisting ceremony on July 18. The holy car procession is one of the important events of the festival, which was held on Tuesday midnight. Hundreds of people gathered to witness the holy car festival.According to sources, aerial crackers were burst in front of the church before the procession commenced. As the crackers were being burst, a spark fell on a huge quantity of crackers stacked a few metres away from the church, which triggered a series of explosions. In this incident, 15 people sustained injuries. They were immediately rushed to the Dindigul Govt Medical College Hospital for treatment.Subsequently, Barath Krishna, 20, of Kalnattanpatti, who sustained severe injuries, was referred to the Govt Rajaji Hospital in Madurai for treatment. Similarly, Karan, 24, of Kalnattanpatti, who sustained severe injuries, was admitted to a private hospital in Dindigul. The Dindigul Taluk police registered a case and are conducting investigations.
Article Summary: As many as 15 people sustained injuries while crackers were burst during the annual festival of St Mary Magdalene Church in Vakkampatti, Dindigul district. The holy car procession is one of the important events of the festival, which was held on Tuesday midnight.

Article Title: Court denies bail to MLA Markandayan
Article Text:
Denied bail to Vilathikulam MLA G V Markandayan,

Tuticorin:

A Tuticorin court on Wednesday denied bail to Vilathikulam MLA G V Markandayan, who was arrested for allegedly making inflammatory remarks against chief minister C Joseph Vijay.Markandayan was booked under three sections after a complaint alleged that he made threatening remarks against the chief minister during a public meeting in Kovilpatti on July 18. He was remanded in judicial custody and lodged in Palayamkottai Central Prison on Monday.The MLA moved a bail petition before Judicial Magistrate-I, Tuticorin, on Wednesday, while police sought an extension of his judicial custody.The court rejected both the bail petition and the police’s request to extend his judicial custody.Speaking to party cadres from the police vehicle, Markandayan said, “The prison conditions are comfortable, and I am happy. There is no need to file a bail petition again,” adding that enduring hardship would help the DMK return to power sooner.
Article Summary: Vilathikulam MLA G V Markandayan was arrested for allegedly making inflammatory remarks against chief minister C Joseph Vijay. He was booked under three sections after a complaint alleged that he made threatening remarks against the chief minister during a public meeting in Kovilpatti on July 18.

Article Title: Kanimozhi visits MLA in prison
Article Text:
DMK Deputy General Secretary and Tuticorin MP Kanimozhi Karunanidhi visited Vilathikulam DMK MLA Markandayan at the Palayamkottai Central Prison on Tuesday

Tirunelveli: DMK deputy general secretary and Tuticorin MP K Kanimozhi met Vilathikulam MLA G V Markandayan, who is lodged in Palayamkottai central prison in a case registered over his alleged remarks against chief minister C Joseph Vijay at a public meeting at Kovilpatti.Markandayan was booked under three sections following a complaint stating he made inflammatory and threatening remarks against the CM at the public meeting on July 18. Kanimozhi enquired about the MLA’s well-being at the prison where he is remanded in judicial custody. District secretaries R Avudaiyappan and Geetha Jeevan, MLAs Anitha R Radhakrishnan, Abdul Wahab and K Karunanidhi, as well as Tuticorin mayor Jegan Periyasamy accompanied the MP.Kanimozhi told reporters that Palayamkottai prison is nothing new to DMK. “Party members view imprisonment here with pride because it is the same jail where M Karunanidhi was once held. Similarly, he (Markandayan) too has accepted his imprisonment with a sense of pride,” she said.
Article Summary: DMK deputy general secretary and Tuticorin MP K Kanimozhi met Vilathikulam DMK MLA G V Markandayan at the Palayamkottai Central Prison on Tuesday. Mark andayan was booked under three sections following a complaint stating he made inflammatory and threatening remarks against the CM at a public meeting.

Article Title: Youth arrested for molesting minor girl
Article Text:
Kerala high court dismissed anticipatory bail plea of Congress councillor

Palakkad

:

A Malappuram native has been arrested in Chittoor, Palakkad in the case of molesting a 16-year-old girl on the pretext of exorcising a ghost.M. Siraj (32) a resident of Vazhayil, Pookkottoor, Malappuram, was arrested by the Chittoor police. Siraj had come to Chittoor on the pretext of looking after a farm. He was staying in Kunnankattupathi, Anchammile, Chittoor. Siraj convinced the girl’s mother that their 16-year-old daughter was possessed by a ghost and that if the curse was not removed, the child would be in danger. He then took the girl to his residence in Kunnankattupathi, Anchammile, to exorcise the curse and allegedly sexually assaulted her there. The girl’s family, filed a complaint with the Chittoor police. Based on the complaint, the Chittoor police took Siraj into custody and registered an arrest. He was produced in court and remanded.
Article Summary: M. Siraj (32) a resident of Vazhayil, Pookkottoor, Malappuram, was arrested by the Chittoor police. He convinced the girl’s mother that their 16-year-old daughter was possessed by a ghost. He then took the girl to his residence in Kunnankattupathi, Anchammile, to exorcise the curse.

Article Title: New Sabarimala tanthri: HC seeks govt’s views
Article Text:
Kochi

:

High court has sought instructions from the state govt on the proposal to appoint Kandararu Brahmadathan, son of Kandararu Rajeevaru, as the tanthri (chief priest) of the Sabarimala temple A bench of Justices V Raja Vijayaraghavan and K V Jayakumar adjourned the matter to July 27 to enable the govt pleader to obtain instructions from the state govt. HC was considering a report submitted by the Sabarimala special commissioner regarding the proposed appointment of Brahmadathan as a tanthri of the temple.Rajeevaru, the present tanthri, was earlier arrayed as an accused in the case related to the alleged misappropriation of gold from the temple. Subsequently, he wrote to the Travancore Devaswom Board seeking to be relieved from the post and requesting that his son be appointed in his place.
Article Summary: High court has sought instructions from the state govt on the proposal to appoint Kandararu Brahmadathan as the tanthri (chief priest) of the Sabarimala temple. Justices V Raja Vijayaraghavan and K V Jayakumar adjourned the matter to July 27 to enable the govt pleader to obtain instructions.
